hypothesis: Among the Si–X alloy candidates identified through the AI framework as exhibiting invariant Li⁺ migration channel sizes across lithiation states, those incorporating alloying elements with higher electronegativity than silicon (e.g., Ge, Sn) will demonstrate a quantifiable correlation between the electronegativity difference (Δχ) of the Si–X bond and the degree of Li⁺ channel stability (as measured by the slope of channel radius versus state of charge), such that alloys with Δχ > 0.3 will exhibit channel radius slopes below 0.3 Å/100% and superior capacity retention after 100 cycles compared to alloys with smaller electronegativity differences, because greater electronegativity contrast strengthens the covalent Si–X sublattice framework against lithiation-induced distortion.
